data.title: ADA Holds Steady as Analyst Eyes Next Leg Up: Can Cardano Still Flip Ethereum in 2025?
data.description:
The cryptocurrency ADA, also known as Cardano, remains stable in its market performance, drawing attention from analysts who are closely monitoring its potential for future growth. As these experts examine the current trends and developments within the cryptocurrency space, the question arises: Is there a possibility that Cardano could surpass Ethereum, the second-largest cryptocurrency by market capitalization, by the year 2025? This inquiry prompts a deeper analysis of both platforms, their innovative technologies, and the broader market dynamics that could influence their standings in the coming years.
source: https://blocknews.com/ada-holds-steady-as-analyst-eyes-next-leg-up-can-cardano-still-flip-ethereum-in-2025/

data.title: Crypto Market Dips—But XRP Still Holding Its Ground? Here are the Price Levels You Need to Watch
data.description:

data.title: JPMorgan Chase: Cryptocurrency market inflows have reached $60 billion so far this year, and investors' interest in altcoins has increased
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to JPMorgan analysts, capital inflows into digital assets have reached $60 billion since the beginning of the year, mainly driven by inflows from crypto funds, active futures trading at CME, and venture capital fundraising activities. Analysts pointed out that since the end of May, capital inflows have increased by nearly 50%, and are expected to easily surpass last year's record. 
Analysts said that the friendlier regulatory environment in the United States is one of the key driving factors. Investors' interest in altcoins is also rising. Among them, Ethereum is the biggest beneficiary due to its dominance in decentralized finance (DeFi) and smart contracts, and it is increasingly being included in corporate treasury allocations along with Bitcoin. More and more asset management companies are interested in launching crypto ETFs based on altcoins and introducing pledge mechanisms. (The Block)

data.title: WOO X was hacked, with losses exceeding $12 million, and the platform has temporarily suspended withdrawals
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Cyvers Alerts posted on the X platform that it detected multiple suspicious transactions involving the trading platform WOO X, covering BTC, ETH, BNB and ARB networks, with an estimated loss of more than $12 million.
On the Ethereum network, a suspicious address received $1 million worth of USDT and additional ETH from WOO's hot wallet. The $1 million USDT was then converted into ETH, and then about $7.3 million in funds were transferred to a new address.
The WOO team has confirmed the incident and said: "We are investigating a restricted incident that occurred on the WOO X platform earlier today." Currently, user funds and transactions are not affected, but the platform has temporarily suspended withdrawals during the investigation.

data.title: OpenAI's GPT-5 Model Set for Early August Release
data.description: According to PANews, OpenAI is preparing to launch its next-generation GPT-5 model in early August. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman recently announced on the X platform that the release is imminent and showcased some of the model's capabilities during a podcast. Altman highlighted GPT-5's ability to quickly answer complex questions, demonstrating the power of AI. The model will be available in various versions, including mini and nano, and will be accessible to external users via API. Additionally, GPT-5 will integrate o3 reasoning capabilities to enhance its intelligence.

data.title: Emmer Seeks Bipartisan Support for Blockchain Regulatory Clarity Act
data.description: According to PANews, during a press conference before the House's August recess, House Majority Whip Tom Emmer announced his efforts to gain more support from Senate Democrats for his Blockchain Regulatory Clarity Act. The proposed legislation aims to clarify that blockchain developers and infrastructure providers, such as miners, validators, and node operators, are not considered money transmitters as long as they do not custody funds. Emmer described the bill as a 'non-partisan issue,' emphasizing the importance of providing clear rules for Americans and innovators. He also expressed openness to Senate adjustments and improvements during the bill's review process.
